## [00:00 - 03:01]

Assisted conversions allows us to understand what role a channel or You know source or medium is playing in the overall marketing mix. So one of the challenges that has been around for you know hundreds of years is which touch points are actually contributing to the sales So, you know, if you go back a hundred years, you know If someone sees a display poster and then they open a magazine and then they cut the coupon and then they go into the store How do you know whether it was actually the magazine or the display poster? Way back before digital was a thing now. It's even more complicated So as you're running multiple channels like if you're at any level with Facebook, you've probably got email running You may have Google ads may have some organic and things like that So, you know, it's important to understand where your different channels are contributing so you can amplify the right areas Now, for example, you might have a scenario where it looks like Facebook just is not working But if you rely on email and email showing amazing revenue, well, guess what email doesn't exist on its own You need to get those emails from somewhere those emails came from Facebook Then you need to absolutely know that as well So in this report, if you're going to conversions multi-channel funnels and assisted conversions What this allows you to do is see Which channels and even write down to campaigns and ad sets are actually contributing Beyond the last click. So as a reminder face Facebook shows last click as far as it can see Google analytics shows last click from as far as it can see. So Google analytics can see traffic from paid search Direct email, etc. Facebook's only really looking at traffic traffic from Facebook and in particular particular Facebook ads So it's a very narrow view. That's why we need Google analytics. Now once you're in this report Assuming you've set up your UTM tracking in the way we do it then go to channel go to other go to You can you can find it in the navigation. I just type in source medium And then here we can have a look at our Facebook traffic here So what I'm what I'm looking to do is look at these two columns here So I can see that the last click Revenue that's generated from paid ads on Facebook as far as Google analytics can see was 52k however An extra sixteen thousand dollars was generated where Facebook assisted It could be the first click it could be one of many clicks in between first and last whatever There is a contribution there as well and this number at the end in particular is of interest The closer that is to zero The lower the contribution is the higher it is the higher the contribution is to other channels as well Now if we click into Facebook CPC here

## [03:01 - 06:04]

I can then see all of my campaign data and I can go a level deeper to see my assists So for example, I'm just looking just glancing through here this campaign here. So DPA catalog sales It generated a hundred and fifty four dollars and five transactions directly However, it contributed another two hundred and fifty dollars Indirectly why is this important? Well, because if we look at our Facebook data It could well be that you know Facebook also says it only generated $150 $200 whatever it is and maybe we would make the wrong decision to pause it because it wasn't profitable But what if two hundred and fifty dollars? As additional assisted conversion actually makes this now look more profitable And it may be the case and this often happens because let's say for example in the case of a catalog ad If someone's already Engaged so for me my warm audiences are people that have engaged And I want to serve up a catalog of a range of products that they might be interested in Let's say they click an ad and then they land on the product page, but they don't buy But then later on my retargeting ad my site retargeting which for me is level three hot actually gets the conversion so I Would I would what I would see in Facebook is that this campaign is performing well this campaign is not performing well and so the wrong decision in this scenario would be to just you know Pause this campaign or reduce budget when in reality it Contributed to generating sales here. So this is an indicator to find those opportunities Where potentially it's overperforming versus maybe what you're seeing and then there's another example here as well This one for example, this is not contributing as heavily and that's kind of telling me that It's likely that most people are buying in the first session So therefore, you know, we've got high last-click conversion value or it's just not contributing as much as others now You know comparatively point two seven is lower than some of these other numbers But it still had an assist at 6k and the thing here is if you imagine in in many cases For me one cold is top of funnel So top of funnel if I've generated 22k and Google analytics has reported that and maybe Facebook has as well But it's generated an extra 6k elsewhere That could be performing better than I think and actually that 6k Could actually be a last click for my hot campaigns or you know clicks into my warm as people click into different ads And as they have different sessions and journeys they are Creating their own path through your site So for example someone could click this ad and then disappear And then they could come back through a retargeting ad and then might come back through another retargeting ad

## [06:04 - 07:04]

So what this tells me is Google analytics has seen this campaign name come up in another $6,000 worth of transaction value So I'll show you how we do that kind of analysis and when we look at our ads data But this is just a report to show you you know how to kind of have a look at that now I can see it by campaign here. You can type in content So you can see it by your ad set name as well And then you can also type in keyword Which is how we look at ad sets to look at it by ad name as well So you can drill down as far as you want to see kind of what's what's working and what's not so you know our 2.2 here is Specifically this ad, you know, there's only there's only one ad running there anyway But that's kind of how you can use this to get a high level idea of what's going on But where the magic happens is top conversion path, which is the next video
